assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration that lacks granular maintenance exclusion types
assertions.reason: The response clearly explains 'No channel' as a legacy configuration and details the lack of granular exclusion types in the comparison table and pain points section

assertions.expectation: Mentions that 'No channel' does not support 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion scope — only the 30-day 'no upgrades' type, AND per-nodepool maintenance exclusion is also limited
assertions.reason: The response explicitly states in the comparison table that 'No channel' lacks 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion and only has the 30-day 'no upgrades' type available. It also notes per-nodepool exclusion limitations

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: The response clearly states in the comparison table and multiple sections that Extended support (24 months) is available on release channels but not on 'No channel'

assertions.expectation: Notes that rollout sequencing (an advanced feature) is not available on 'No channel'
assertions.reason: The comparison table explicitly shows rollout sequencing as available on release channels but not on 'No channel', and it's mentioned as an expected benefit post-migration

assertions.expectation: Recommends Regular or Stable channel as the closest migration target (legacy behavior matches Stable for minors, Regular for patches)
assertions.reason: The response primarily recommends Extended channel as the main migration target and only mentions Regular channel as an alternative. It does not mention Stable channel at all or explain the behavioral matching between 'No channel' and Regular/Stable

assertions.expectation: Mentions Extended channel as a migration path for customers who want maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: The response recommends Extended channel as the primary migration path and explains it provides maximum flexibility with 24-month support and persistent exclusions

assertions.expectation: Warns about exclusion translation: when moving from 'No channel' to release channels, some exclusions don't translate 1:1 and may be ignored — only 'no_upgrades' type translates between the two. Recommends adding a temporary 'no upgrades' exclusion during the transition
assertions.reason: The response includes explicit warnings about coordination during maintenance windows and provides commands to apply temporary 'no upgrades' exclusions before migration to avoid unexpected auto-upgrades

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Multiple gcloud commands are provided throughout the response for changing release channels, including specific examples for Extended, Regular, and migration scenarios
The response does not reference or link to the official GKE documentation at cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/release-channels
